items.reason: The PR replaces log_rate_limit with three sample-rate knobs but the updated section says the default is fully backward compatible even though the design/code remove the old flag/env/API with no mapping.
The PR removes DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT from the client environment variable table, matching the worker-authoritative sampling design.
The dscli configuration table replaces log_rate_limit with request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, and diagnostic_sample_rate plus the derivation rule.
The new design document describes LogSampler behavior, hot-path constraints, config propagation, derivation, and explicit removal of log_rate_limit compatibility.
The context index adds routing entries for the new log sampler design.
This existing logging design still documents log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, and LogRateLimiter client sync, which the PR replaces with LogSampler/sample-rate config.
This trace context doc still says request traces create decisions through local log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter, stale after the PR changes request decisions to LogSampler/request_sample_rate.
The slow PLOG design still instructs bypassing LogRateLimiter/log_rate_limit, but the PR removes LogRateLimiter and changes PLOG handling to diagnostic sampling semantics.
The Get slow-log summary still frames its behavior around log_rate_limit request sampling, which is stale after the PR replaces the sampler and diagnostic retention rules.
